title: The effects of sham hypnosis techniques
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 52 participants (39 females) investigating the effects of sham hypnosis techniques using a 2×2 balanced placebo design. Participants underwent four experimental conditions involving conventional and placebo (unconventional) hypnotic inductions presented as either hypnosis or relaxation, with brain activity recorded using 64-channel EEG during baseline and experimental blocks. The study examines how expectancy and framing influence the neurophysiological response to hypnotic inductions.
methods_description: Brain electrophysiological activity was recorded from 52 participants using a 64-channel EasyCap system from Brain Products. The protocol consisted of 5 minutes of closed-eyes rest (Pre-hypnosis Baseline), four experimental blocks with randomized exposure to conventional or unconventional hypnotic inductions presented as either hypnosis or control/relaxation, and 5 minutes of closed-eyes rest (Post-hypnosis Baseline).

affiliations.name: Institute of Psychology ELTE, Eötvös Lorand University  Budapest Hungary
